agent_output: {"domain_analysis":{"business_context":"Leave management module within an HR software system. Governs how employees accrue, request, consume, and carry over paid leave. The rules encode both employee entitlements and organizational controls to prevent abuse and ensure operational continuity.","bounded_context":"Leave Management","ubiquitous_language":[{"term":"Leave Balance","definition":"The current number of accrued leave days available to an employee, calculated as total accrued minus total consumed. Must never be negative."},{"term":"Accrual","definition":"The process by which an employee earns 1.5 leave days for each month worked under an active contract."},{"term":"Leave Request","definition":"A formal submission by an employee to take one or more days of leave, subject to balance availability, advance notice, and manager approval."},{"term":"Medical Leave","definition":"A special category of leave that may be submitted retroactively (up to 5 calendar days after the absence) when accompanied by a doctor's note, exempt from the standard 3-working-day advance notice rule."},{"term":"Working Days","definition":"Business days excluding weekends and company-observed holidays, used to measure the advance notice period for leave requests."},{"term":"Contract End Date","definition":"The termination date of an employee's employment contract. Leave requests must not extend beyond this date."},{"term":"Carry-Over","definition":"The process at calendar year-end where up to 10 days of unused leave balance transfer to the next year; any excess is forfeited."},{"term":"Forfeiture","definition":"The permanent loss of accrued leave days exceeding the 10-day carry-over cap at calendar year-end."},{"term":"Doctor's Note","definition":"A medical certificate required to validate a retroactive medical leave request."}]},"business_rules":[{"id":"BR-1","rule":"Employees accrue 1.5 days of leave per month worked under an active contract.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"Accrual rate is a policy parameter that HR leadership could adjust (e.g., to 2.0 days). It does not represent a structural integrity constraint.","testable_assertion":"After each completed month of employment, the employee's accrued leave total increases by exactly 1.5 days."},{"id":"BR-2","rule":"An employee's leave balance must never go below zero. Leave cannot be taken against unaccrued days.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"A negative balance would mean the employee consumed leave that does not exist — this is a data integrity violation representing a temporal impossibility (using something before it is earned).","testable_assertion":"For any employee at any point in time: leave_balance >= 0 AND requested_days <= available_balance at time of approval."},{"id":"BR-3","rule":"Non-medical leave requests must be submitted at least 3 working days before the first day of requested leave.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"The advance notice period is a policy preference that management could shorten or lengthen without corrupting system state.","testable_assertion":"Given a non-medical leave request, the number of working days between submission date and leave start date must be >= 3; otherwise the system rejects the request."},{"id":"BR-4","rule":"Medical leave can be submitted retroactively within 5 calendar days of the absence, provided a doctor's note is attached.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"The retroactive window and documentation requirement are policy decisions, not structural constraints.","testable_assertion":"A medical leave request is accepted if: (a) leave_type = MEDICAL, (b) a doctor's note document is attached, and (c) submission_date - last_day_of_absence <= 5 calendar days."},{"id":"BR-5","rule":"Only the employee's manager may approve or reject a leave request.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"Authorization control is a security invariant — allowing unauthorized actors to approve leave would break the chain of command and constitute unauthorized state mutation.","testable_assertion":"The approver/rejector of a leave request must hold the MANAGER role for the requesting employee's reporting line."},{"id":"BR-6","rule":"Rejected leave does not consume leave balance. Balance is only debited upon approval.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"Consuming balance on rejection would corrupt the employee's entitlement — this is a state integrity rule that ensures balance accurately reflects actual approved absences.","testable_assertion":"When a leave request transitions to REJECTED status, the employee's leave balance remains unchanged from its value before the request was submitted."},{"id":"BR-7","rule":"A leave request must not span across (extend beyond) the employee's contract end date.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"An employee cannot be on approved company leave when they are no longer under contract — this is a logical impossibility that would create phantom obligations.","testable_assertion":"For any leave request: last_day_of_leave <= employee.contract_end_date; otherwise the system rejects the request."},{"id":"BR-8","rule":"At calendar year-end, unused leave above 10 days is forfeited. A maximum of 10 days carries over to January 1 of the next year.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"The 10-day cap is a policy threshold that could be changed by HR leadership without violating system integrity.","testable_assertion":"On January 1, carried_over_balance = MIN(previous_year_end_balance, 10). Forfeited = MAX(previous_year_end_balance - 10, 0)."}],"acceptance_criteria":[{"id":"AC-1","given":"An employee has worked a full calendar month under an active contract","when":"The monthly accrual process runs","then":"The employee's leave balance increases by exactly 1.5 days"},{"id":"AC-2","given":"An employee has a leave balance of 3 days","when":"They submit a non-medical leave request for 4 days","then":"The request is rejected with reason 'insufficient balance' and the balance remains 3 days"},{"id":"AC-3","given":"An employee has a leave balance of 5 days","when":"They submit a non-medical leave request for 3 days starting tomorrow (1 working day away)","then":"The request is rejected with reason 'insufficient advance notice — minimum 3 working days required'"},{"id":"AC-4","given":"An employee has a leave balance of 5 days","when":"They submit a non-medical leave request for 3 days starting 4 working days from now","then":"The request is accepted and enters PENDING_APPROVAL status"},{"id":"AC-5","given":"An employee was absent due to illness 3 calendar days ago","when":"They submit a medical leave request with a valid doctor's note attached","then":"The request is accepted despite being retroactive and enters PENDING_APPROVAL status"},{"id":"AC-6","given":"An employee was absent due to illness 6 calendar days ago","when":"They submit a medical leave request with a doctor's note","then":"The request is rejected with reason 'retroactive medical leave must be submitted within 5 calendar days of absence'"},{"id":"AC-7","given":"An employee submits a medical leave request retroactively within 5 days","when":"No doctor's note is attached","then":"The request is rejected with reason 'doctor's note required for retroactive medical leave'"},{"id":"AC-8","given":"A leave request is in PENDING_APPROVAL status","when":"The employee's manager approves it","then":"The request status changes to APPROVED and the employee's balance is debited by the number of leave days"},{"id":"AC-9","given":"A leave request is in PENDING_APPROVAL status","when":"The employee's manager rejects it","then":"The request status changes to REJECTED and the employee's leave balance remains unchanged"},{"id":"AC-10","given":"An employee's contract ends on March 15","when":"They submit a leave request for March 13–17","then":"The request is rejected with reason 'leave request cannot extend beyond contract end date'"},{"id":"AC-11","given":"An employee has 14 unused leave days on December 31","when":"The annual carry-over process runs on January 1","then":"The employee's new balance is 10 days and 4 days are recorded as forfeited"},{"id":"AC-12","given":"An employee has 8 unused leave days on December 31","when":"The annual carry-over process runs on January 1","then":"The employee's new balance is 8 days and 0 days are forfeited"}],"edge_cases":[{"scenario":"Employee submits leave request on exactly the 3rd working day before leave start","expected_behavior":"Request is accepted — the rule is 'at least 3 working days' so the boundary is inclusive (3 working days satisfies the requirement)."},{"scenario":"Employee's contract ends on a weekend and they request leave up to the preceding Friday","expected_behavior":"Request is valid — the last leave day (Friday) is before the contract end date."},{"scenario":"Medical leave spans a weekend (e.g., Friday absence, submission on Wednesday = 5 calendar days)","expected_behavior":"The 5-day retroactive window is measured in calendar days from the last day of absence, so Wednesday submission for a Friday absence (5 calendar days later) is still within the window."},{"scenario":"Leave request is approved, then the employee's contract end date is shortened to fall within the leave period","expected_behavior":"System should flag the conflict and require cancellation or modification of the approved leave. Approved leave beyond the new contract end date is invalid."},{"scenario":"Employee accrues a partial month (e.g., starts mid-month)","expected_behavior":"Spec says 'per month worked' — a partial first/last month accrual policy needs to be defined (pro-rata or full-month-only). This is an ambiguity in the spec."},{"scenario":"Carry-over runs but employee also has a pending leave request that, if approved, would reduce balance below 10","expected_behavior":"Carry-over should be calculated on the actual balance at year-end. Pending (unapproved) requests should not reduce the balance used for carry-over calculation since only approved leave debits balance."},{"scenario":"Manager attempts to approve a request but the employee's balance has been consumed by another approved request in the interim","expected_behavior":"System must re-validate balance at approval time, not just at submission time. If insufficient, approval must be blocked."},{"scenario":"Employee submits medical leave retroactively on exactly the 5th calendar day after absence","expected_behavior":"Accepted — 'within 5 days' is inclusive of day 5."}],"business_impact":{"financial":"Incorrect accrual or carry-over logic could result in employees taking more leave than entitled, creating unbudgeted labor cost exposure. Conversely, under-accrual creates compliance risk and potential labor disputes.","operational":"Leave management directly affects workforce planning and scheduling. Bugs in advance notice enforcement or approval workflows could lead to unstaffed shifts. The carry-over forfeiture rule incentivizes employees to use leave within the year, smoothing operational impact.","user":"Employees interact with this system for a core entitlement. Errors in balance calculation or unfair rejections erode trust in the HR platform. Clear, predictable rules improve employee satisfaction.","risk":"Non-compliance with labor laws around leave entitlements is a legal risk. The spec does not reference jurisdiction-specific statutory minimums, which could result in forfeiture rules violating local labor law. Medical leave handling must also comply with health data privacy regulations.","stakeholders_affected":[{"group":"Employees","impact":"Direct users of the leave system — incorrect balances or unfair rejections affect their entitlements and work-life balance."},{"group":"Managers","impact":"Responsible for approval workflow — need clear visibility into team availability and leave balances to make informed decisions."},{"group":"HR / People Operations","impact":"Own the policy configuration, handle disputes, ensure legal compliance, and run year-end carry-over processes."},{"group":"Payroll","impact":"Downstream consumer of approved leave data — errors propagate into incorrect salary calculations if leave affects pay."},{"group":"Finance","impact":"Leave accrual creates a financial liability on the balance sheet; incorrect calculations affect financial reporting."}]},"confidence":{"level":78,"reasoning":"The spec is well-defined with clear, testable rules. No contradictions detected. Minor ambiguities exist around partial-month accrual and boundary conditions (inclusive vs exclusive on deadlines), but these are typical implementation details rather than fundamental spec flaws. The absence of jurisdiction-specific legal context slightly reduces confidence.","high_confidence_areas":["Non-negative balance invariant","Accrual rate and mechanism","Advance notice rule and medical leave exemption","Rejected leave does not consume balance","Carry-over cap of 10 days with forfeiture","Contract end date boundary for leave requests"],"low_confidence_areas":["Partial month accrual policy (pro-rata vs full month only) not specified","Whether 'within 5 days' and 'at least 3 working days' boundaries are inclusive or exclusive","Jurisdiction-specific labor law compliance for forfeiture rules","Whether carry-over forfeiture applies to all leave types or only standard leave","What happens to approved future leave when contract end date changes","Whether balance check occurs at submission, approval, or both"],"assumptions":["The 5-day retroactive window for medical leave is measured in calendar days, not working days, based on the spec not qualifying it as 'working days' unlike the advance notice rule","Balance is debited at approval time, not at submission time, since the spec states rejected leave does not consume balance (implying approval triggers the debit)","The 3-working-day advance notice and 5-day retroactive window boundaries are inclusive","Accrual happens per completed calendar month — partial months are not addressed and assumed to need clarification","The doctor's note is a required attachment, not merely a reference — the system must enforce its presence"]},"escalations":[],"rejection_reasons":[]}

Leave requests must not extend beyond this date."},{"term":"Carry-Over","definition":"The process at calendar year-end where up to 10 days of unused leave balance transfer to the next year; any excess is forfeited."},{"term":"Forfeiture","definition":"The permanent loss of accrued leave days exceeding the 10-day carry-over cap at calendar year-end."},{"term":"Doctor's Note","definition":"A medical certificate required to validate a retroactive medical leave request."}]},"business_rules":[{"id":"BR-1","rule":"Employees accrue 1.5 days of leave per month worked under an active contract.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"Accrual rate is a policy parameter that HR leadership could adjust (e.g., to 2.0 days). Balance is only debited upon approval.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"Consuming balance on rejection would corrupt the employee's entitlement — this is a state integrity rule that ensures balance accurately reflects actual approved absences.","testable_assertion":"When a leave request transitions to REJECTED status, the employee's leave balance remains unchanged from its value before the request was submitted."},{"id":"BR-7","rule":"A leave request must not span across (extend beyond) the employee's contract end date.","invariant":true,"invariant_justification":"An employee cannot be on approved company leave when they are no longer under contract — this is a logical impossibility that would create phantom obligations.","testable_assertion":"For any leave request: last_day_of_leave <= employee.contract_end_date; otherwise the system rejects the request."},{"id":"BR-8","rule":"At calendar year-end, unused leave above 10 days is forfeited. A maximum of 10 days carries over to January 1 of the next year.","invariant":false,"invariant_justification":"The 10-day cap is a policy threshold that could be changed by HR leadership without violating system integrity.","testable_assertion":"On January 1, carried_over_balance = MIN(previous_year_end_balance, 10). Forfeited = MAX(previous_year_end_balance - 10, 0)."}],"acceptance_criteria":[{"id":"AC-1","given":"An employee has worked a full calendar month under an active contract","when":"The monthly accrual process runs","then":"The employee's leave balance increases by exactly 1.5 days"},{"id":"AC-2","given":"An employee has a leave balance of 3 days","when":"They submit a non-medical leave request for 4 days","then":"The request is rejected with reason 'insufficient balance' and the balance remains 3 days"},{"id":"AC-3","given":"An employee has a leave balance of 5 days","when":"They submit a non-medical leave request for 3 days starting tomorrow (1 working day away)","then":"The request is rejected with reason 'insufficient advance notice — minimum 3 working days required'"},{"id":"AC-4","given":"An employee has a leave balance of 5 days","when":"They submit a non-medical leave request for 3 days starting 4 working days from now","then":"The request is accepted and enters PENDING_APPROVAL status"},{"id":"AC-5","given":"An employee was absent due to illness 3 calendar days ago","when":"They submit a medical leave request with a valid doctor's note 13–17","then":"The request is rejected with reason 'leave request cannot extend beyond contract end date'"},{"id":"AC-11","given":"An employee has 14 unused leave days on December 31","when":"The annual carry-over process runs on January 1","then":"The employee's new balance is 10 days and 4 days are recorded as forfeited"},{"id":"AC-12","given":"An employee has 8 unused leave days on December 31","when":"The annual carry-over process runs on January 1","then":"The employee's new balance is 8 days and 0 days are forfeited"}],"edge_cases":[{"scenario":"Employee submits leave request on exactly the 3rd working day before leave start","expected_behavior":"Request is accepted — the rule is 'at least 3 working days' so the boundary is inclusive (3 working days satisfies the requirement)."},{"scenario":"Employee's contract ends on a weekend and they request leave up to the preceding Friday","expected_behavior":"Request is valid — the last leave day (Friday) is before the contract end date."},{"scenario":"Medical leave spans a weekend (e.g., Friday absence, submission on Wednesday = 5 calendar days)","expected_behavior":"The 5-day retroactive window is measured in calendar days from the last day of absence, so Wednesday submission for a Friday absence (5 calendar days later) is still within the window."},{"scenario":"Leave request is approved, then the This is an ambiguity in the spec."},{"scenario":"Carry-over runs but employee also has a pending leave request that, if approved, would reduce balance below 10","expected_behavior":"Carry-over should be calculated on the actual balance at year-end. Pending (unapproved) requests should not reduce the balance used for carry-over calculation since only approved leave debits balance."},{"scenario":"Manager attempts to approve a request but the employee's balance has been consumed by another approved request in the interim","expected_behavior":"System must re-validate balance at approval time, not just at submission time. If insufficient, approval must be blocked."},{"scenario":"Employee submits medical leave retroactively on exactly the 5th calendar day after absence","expected_behavior":"Accepted — 'within 5 days' is inclusive of day 5."}],"business_impact":{"financial":"Incorrect accrual or carry-over logic could result in employees taking more leave than entitled, creating unbudgeted labor cost exposure. Conversely, under-accrual creates compliance risk and potential labor disputes.","operational":"Leave management directly affects workforce planning and scheduling. Bugs in advance notice enforcement or approval workflows could lead to unstaffed shifts. The carry-over forfeiture rule incentivizes employees to use leave within the year, smoothing operational impact.","user":"Employees interact with this system for a core entitlement. Errors in balance calculation or unfair rejections erode trust in the HR platform. Clear, predictable rules improve employee satisfaction.","risk":"Non-compliance with labor laws around leave entitlements is a legal risk. The spec does not reference jurisdiction-specific statutory minimums, which could result in forfeiture rules violating local labor law. Medical leave handling must also comply with health data privacy regulations.","stakeholders_affected":[{"group":"Employees","impact":"Direct users of the leave system — incorrect balances or unfair rejections affect their entitlements and work-life balance."},{"group":"Managers","impact":"Responsible for approval workflow — need clear visibility into team availability and leave balances to make informed decisions."},{"group":"HR / People Operations","impact":"Own the policy configuration, handle disputes, ensure legal compliance, and run year-end carry-over processes."},{"group":"Payroll","impact":"Downstream consumer of approved leave data — errors propagate into incorrect salary calculations if leave affects pay."},{"group":"Finance","impact":"Leave accrual creates a financial liability on the balance sheet; incorrect calculations affect financial reporting."}]},"confidence":{"level":78,"reasoning":"The spec is well-defined with clear, testable rules. No contradictions detected. Minor ambiguities exist around partial-month accrual and boundary conditions (inclusive vs exclusive on deadlines), but these are typical implementation details rather than fundamental spec flaws. The absence of jurisdiction-specific legal context slightly reduces confidence.","high_confidence_areas":["Non-negative balance invariant","Accrual rate and mechanism","Advance notice rule and medical leave exemption","Rejected leave does not consume balance","Carry-over cap of 10 days with forfeiture","Contract end date boundary for leave requests"],"low_confidence_areas":["Partial month accrual policy (pro-rata vs full month only) not specified","Whether 'within 5 days' and 'at least 3 working days' boundaries are inclusive or exclusive","Jurisdiction-specific labor law compliance for forfeiture rules","Whether carry-over forfeiture applies to all leave types or only standard leave","What happens to approved future leave when contract end date changes","Whether balance check occurs at submission, approval, or both"],"assumptions":["The 5-day retroactive window for medical leave is measured in calendar days, not working days, based on the spec not qualifying it as 'working days' unlike the advance notice rule","Balance is debited at approval time, not at submission time, since the spec states rejected leave does not consume balance (implying approval triggers the debit)","The 3-working-day advance notice and 5-day retroactive window boundaries are inclusive","Accrual happens per completed calendar month — partial months are not addressed and assumed to need clarification","The doctor's note is a required attachment, not merely a reference — the system must enforce its presence"]},"escalations":[],"rejection_reasons":[]}
